input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule F.5.7.5 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.5.7.5
            TITLE: the front hoop must be no more than 250 mm forward of the steering wheel.

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.7.5: The Front Hoop must be no more than 250 mm forward of the steering wheel.
F.5.7.5 The Front Hoop must be no more than 250 mm forward of the steering wheel.  
This distance is measured horizontally, on the vehicle centerline, from the rear surface of the 
Front Hoop to the forward most surface of the steering wheel rim with the steering in the 
straight ahead position.

intermediate_steps.yolo_config_raw: {"detection_model": "YOLOv26", "slice_height": 4000, "slice_width": 640, "overlap_height_ratio": 0.9, "overlap_width_ratio": 0.9, "keywords": ["dimension line", "dimension arrow", "numerical value", "vehicle centerline", "steering wheel rim"]}
intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, numerical value, steering wheel rim

final_answer:
Explanation: The rule F.5.7.5 requires measuring the horizontal distance from the rear surface of the Front Hoop to the forward most surface of the steering wheel rim. While the engineering drawing provides several dimensions related to the front structure (e.g., 0.37 and 0.53), it does not explicitly label the single dimension representing the distance from the rear surface of the Front Hoop to the forward most surface of the steering wheel rim. As per instructions, if a dimension is not explicitly shown, compliance is assumed.

Answer: Yes
